description: Behavioral test corpus for the LLM validator stack. Each entry is a (facts, expected_validator_outcome, allow_causal) tuple meant to exercise the validator against canonical real-shaped inputs and outputs. Run weekly against the live Groq endpoint to detect prompt drift; run inline (mocked) on every PR.

cases.model_output: On weekends, observations are associated with an effect size of 0.42 (p=0.012). The pattern was observed in 240 records, approximately 83% of the segment.
High-volume traffic appears to drives an increase in delivery time (effect 0.30, p=0.04, observed in 100 records). The pattern is associated with peak hours.
High-volume traffic causes an increase in delivery time (effect 0.30, p=0.04). The pattern was observed in 100 records.
The pattern is associated with 40% of the sample (effect 0.40, p=0.012). The complementary 60% does not show this signal.
Temperature-high segment was tested but did not pass stability validation (effect 0.05, p=0.31). The signal was observed in 80 records, approximately 20% of the cohort.
Morning hours are associated with a moderate effect on the outcome (effect 0.18, p=0.008). Across 320 records, the segment represents 55% of the data; the remaining 45% does not show this pattern.
This segment shows an effect of 1.95 — well outside the range of any input fact.
This always indicates a delivery delay (effect 0.4, p=0.01).
Slowdowns may be due to high traffic (effect 0.4, p=0.01); the pattern is associated with peak hours, present in approximately 50% of records over 100 samples.
Slowdowns are due to high traffic (effect 0.4, p=0.01). The pattern shows in 100 records, exactly 50% of the cohort.
